description: Questions about the Happy Birthday song, birthday music traditions from around the world, music education, auditory development, and cultural musicology of birthday celebrations.

research_sources.title: Happy Birthday Song Copyright Lawsuit: The Strange History of a Song
History of the Happy Birthday Song
Birthday Celebrations Around the World: Cultural Traditions and Customs
Birthday Songs Around The World - Mama Lisa's World
Children's brains develop faster with music training - USC Today
How musical training affects cognitive development: rhythm, reward and other modulating variables
The Importance of Music in Early Childhood - UMD College of Education
Music Interventions and Child Development: A Critical Review
Why Music Is So Important to Different Cultures Around The World

questions.question: What is the name of the song that people sing to celebrate someone's birthday?
questions.answer: Happy Birthday (to You)

questions.question: In the Happy Birthday song, we sing 'Happy Birthday to ___'—what word goes in the blank?
questions.answer: you

questions.question: Do you usually sing Happy Birthday fast or slow?
questions.answer: slow

questions.question: What do you do after singing Happy Birthday to someone—cut cake or blow out candles?
questions.answer: blow out candles

questions.question: What is the last line of the Happy Birthday song that wishes someone well?
questions.answer: Happy Birthday to you (or 'and many more')

questions.question: The Happy Birthday song was written by two American sisters—are they named Smith or Jones?
questions.answer: Smith (Patty Smith Hill and Mildred Hill)

questions.question: The Happy Birthday song was originally written for a classroom and had different lyrics—was it for teaching math or greeting students?
questions.answer: greeting students (it was called 'Good Morning to All')

questions.question: In Mexico, the birthday song is called 'Las Mañanitas'—what does this mean in English?
questions.answer: The Little Mornings (or 'The Early Mornings')

questions.question: In Poland, people sing a birthday song called 'Stolat'—does this song ask someone to live for 100 years or 50 years?
questions.answer: 100 years

questions.question: What country celebrates birthdays with a song called 'Mravalzamier' which means 'may you live for many years'?
questions.answer: Georgia (the Republic of Georgia)

questions.question: When you play music in your ears, which body part is receiving the sound?
questions.answer: ears (or auditory system)

questions.question: Does singing happy birthday help babies learn language better or just for fun?
questions.answer: helps babies learn language better

questions.question: In Brazil, do people blow candles out on the first try or take many tries?
questions.answer: take many tries (as part of the fun)

questions.question: What instrument does a music box often play when celebrating something?
questions.answer: a little bell or tinkling melody

questions.question: When people sing Happy Birthday together, does music help them feel more connected or less connected?
questions.answer: more connected

questions.question: What two American sisters wrote the melody for the Happy Birthday song in 1893?
questions.answer: Patty Smith Hill and Mildred Hill

questions.question: What was the original title of the Happy Birthday melody when it was first written for classrooms?
questions.answer: Good Morning to All

questions.question: In what year was the Happy Birthday song's original melody composed?
Where were Patty and Mildred Hill teaching when they created the Good Morning to All song that became Happy Birthday?
questions.answer: kindergarten (or an elementary school in Kentucky)

questions.question: According to the 1998 Guinness World Records, is Happy Birthday the first or second most recognized song in English?
questions.answer: first

questions.question: Into how many languages has the Happy Birthday song been translated?
questions.answer: at least 18 languages

questions.question: What Mexican birthday song is traditionally sung in the morning and sometimes includes pushing faces into cake?
questions.answer: Las Mañanitas

questions.question: What is the Polish birthday song that wishes someone to live for 100 years called?
questions.answer: Stolat

questions.question: In Georgia (the country), they sing a birthday song called Mravalzamier—what does it mean?
questions.answer: may you live for many years

questions.question: Do about 10%, 50%, or 90% of birthday celebrations worldwide use the Happy Birthday melody with different lyrics?
What is ethnomusicology the study of?
questions.answer: the relationship between music and culture

questions.question: When children learn a musical instrument, does their auditory pathway in the brain mature faster or slower than other kids?
questions.answer: faster

questions.question: According to USC research, how long does it take for music training to speed up a child's auditory system development?
questions.answer: approximately two years

questions.question: Music training in children is associated with improvement in what ability needed for reading?
questions.answer: language and reading (or phonetic discrimination)

questions.question: In India, birthday celebrations are known for being colorful and include music and what other art form?
questions.answer: dancing

questions.question: In Egypt, people celebrate birthdays with what two activities: singing and what else?
questions.answer: dancing

questions.question: What instrument sets the rhythm at Ghanaian birthday celebrations?
questions.answer: drums

questions.question: Music serves what role in birthday celebrations across different cultures?
questions.answer: creating unity, evoking emotion, fostering social interaction

questions.question: Is Belgium known for using the standard Happy Birthday tune or creating something different?
questions.answer: creating something different

questions.question: The Netherlands, like Belgium, is known for creating a unique birthday song or following the Happy Birthday tune?
questions.answer: creating a unique birthday song

questions.question: Who were the composers of the Happy Birthday melody, and what were their surnames?
questions.answer: Patty Smith Hill and Mildred Hill

questions.question: What was the original purpose and title of the melody that became Happy Birthday to You?
questions.answer: A kindergarten greeting song titled 'Good Morning to All'

questions.answer: 1988, for approximately $25 million

questions.question: What was the estimated annual revenue from Happy Birthday licensing fees before the copyright was challenged?
questions.answer: more than $2 million per year

questions.question: In what year did a federal judge declare that the Warner/Chappell copyright on Happy Birthday was invalid?
questions.answer: 2015 (September 2015)

questions.question: What did the federal judge rule about the Warner/Chappell copyright—that it covered the song lyrics and melody or only a specific arrangement?
questions.answer: only a specific piano arrangement

questions.question: In 2016, how much did Warner/Chappell settle for after losing their copyright claim on Happy Birthday?
questions.answer: $14 million

questions.question: What is the current copyright status of the song Happy Birthday to You?
questions.answer: public domain
